title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: mrpheus R package raw polysomnography (PSG) signal analysis. ingests EDF/EDF+ recordings, detects sleep events, computes spectral features, stages 30-second epoch using pre-trained LightGBM model ported YASA (Vallat & Walker, 2021). name spelled Morpheus carries silent m, pronounced Orpheus — carrying myths . Morpheus, god dreams, gives package subject; Orpheus, descended underworld navigate unconscious, gives spirit. mrpheus part Circadia Lab R ecosystem:

title: Getting started with mrpheus
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("circadia-bio/mrpheus")

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: automatic sleep staging model bundled package default — must extracted YASA installation: saves inst/models/yasa_staging.txt — cross-language LightGBM model loads identically R. See ?stage_epochs details.
code: pip install yasa lightgbm python data-raw/fetch_yasa_model.py

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: entry point read_edf(), wraps edfReader consistent output formatting: Pass only_header = TRUE inspect channels without loading signal data:
code: library(mrpheus)  rec <- read_edf("recordings/psg_001.edf") rec ## mrpheus EDF recording ## ℹ Path: recordings/psg_001.edf ## ℹ Duration: 7.98 hours ## ℹ Channels: 14 ## # A tibble: 14 × 3 ##   label              sample_rate transducer ##   <chr>                    <dbl> <chr> ## 1 EEG Fpz-Cz                 100 AgAgCl electrode ## 2 EEG Pz-Oz                  100 AgAgCl electrode ## 3 EOG horizontal              100 AgAgCl electrode ## ... hdr <- read_edf("recordings/psg_001.edf", only_header = TRUE)

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: prepare_psg() segments recording 30-second epochs, classifies channels type (EEG, EOG, EMG, ECG, RESP), flags flat bad channels: Channel classification uses regex patterns can overridden:
code: psg <- prepare_psg(rec) psg ## mrpheus PSG ## ℹ Epochs: 958 × 30 s ## ℹ Recording: 7.98 h ## # A tibble: 14 × 4 ##   label              type  sample_rate   bad ##   <chr>              <chr>       <dbl> <lgl> ## 1 EEG Fpz-Cz         EEG           100 FALSE ## 2 EEG Pz-Oz          EEG           100 FALSE ## 3 EOG horizontal      EOG           100 FALSE ## ... psg <- prepare_psg(rec,   eeg_pattern  = "EEG|C3|C4|F3|F4|O1|O2|Fpz|Pz",   resp_pattern = "Thor|Abdo|Flow|SpO2|airflow" )

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: detect_artifacts() flags epochs excessive amplitude high-frequency (muscle) contamination:
code: art <- detect_artifacts(psg) ## ℹ Artefact epochs: 12 / 958 (1.3%) # Inspect flagged epochs art[art$artefact, ] ## # A tibble: 12 × 5 ##   epoch artefact reason    peak_to_peak_uv hf_power_db ##   <int> <lgl>    <chr>               <dbl>       <dbl> ## 1    34 TRUE     amplitude            524.        -18.2 ## 2    87 TRUE     high_freq            312.          6.4 ## ...

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: compute_band_power() estimates PSD via Welch’s method integrates within standard EEG bands per epoch per channel:
code: bp <- compute_band_power(psg, relative = TRUE) bp ## # A tibble: 958 × 9 ##   epoch channel    delta theta alpha sigma  beta gamma total_power ##   <int> <chr>      <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl> ## 1     1 EEG Fpz-Cz 0.412 0.183 0.142 0.089 0.112 0.062          NA ## ...

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: compute_spectrogram() returns time × frequency power matrix:
code: sg <- compute_spectrogram(psg, channel = "EEG Fpz-Cz", db = TRUE) sg ## mrpheus spectrogram ## ℹ Channel: EEG Fpz-Cz ## ℹ Epochs: 958 ## ℹ Frequency range: 0-40 Hz (161 bins) ## ℹ Units: dB

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: compute_spindles() detects sleep spindles via RMS envelope thresholding sigma band (11–16 Hz):
code: sp <- compute_spindles(psg, channel = "EEG Fpz-Cz") ## ✔ Detected 312 spindles in channel 'EEG Fpz-Cz'. sp ## # A tibble: 312 × 7 ##   epoch start_s end_s duration_s peak_freq_hz rms_uv channel ##   <int>   <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>        <dbl>  <dbl> <chr> ## 1   142    4.23  5.11       0.88         13.2    18.4 EEG Fpz-Cz ## ...

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: compute_slow_oscillations() uses zero-crossing detection delta band (0.5–2 Hz), following Mölle et al. (2002):
code: so <- compute_slow_oscillations(psg, channel = "EEG Fpz-Cz") ## ✔ Detected 188 slow oscillations in channel 'EEG Fpz-Cz'.

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: stage_epochs() stages epoch using pre-trained LightGBM model. Pass artefact table exclude flagged epochs: Stages follow AASM nomenclature: W, N1, N2, N3, REM. Artefact epochs coded NA. Note: stage_epochs() requires staging model inst/models/yasa_staging.txt. Feature parity YASA’s Python pipeline still validated — see source .extract_staging_features() current implementation status.
code: staging <- stage_epochs(   psg,   eeg_channel = "EEG Fpz-Cz",   eog_channel = "EOG horizontal",   emg_channel = "EMG chin",   artefacts   = art ) staging ## # A tibble: 958 × 7 ##   epoch stage prob_W prob_N1 prob_N2 prob_N3 prob_REM ##   <int> <chr>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl> ## 1     1 W      0.921   0.042   0.022   0.008    0.007 ## 2     2 W      0.874   0.081   0.031   0.007    0.007 ## 3     3 N1     0.312   0.448   0.185   0.032    0.023 ## ...

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: export_hypnogram() converts staging output hypnor_hypnogram object downstream architecture analysis:
code: hypnogram <- export_hypnogram(   staging,   epoch_s        = 30,   start_time     = rec$header$startTime,   participant_id = "P001" )  # hypnor functions now work directly on the hypnogram hypnor::plot_hypnogram(hypnogram) hypnor::compute_architecture(hypnogram)

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: Putting together:
code: library(mrpheus)  # 1. Ingest rec      <- read_edf("recordings/psg_001.edf") psg      <- prepare_psg(rec)  # 2. Quality art      <- detect_artifacts(psg)  # 3. Spectral bp       <- compute_band_power(psg, relative = TRUE) sg       <- compute_spectrogram(psg, channel = "EEG Fpz-Cz")  # 4. Events sp       <- compute_spindles(psg) so       <- compute_slow_oscillations(psg)  # 5. Stage staging  <- stage_epochs(psg, artefacts = art)  # 6. Hand off hypnogram <- export_hypnogram(staging, start_time = rec$header$startTime)

title: Getting started with mrpheus
text: Vallat, R., & Walker, M. P. (2021). open-source, high-performance tool automated sleep staging. eLife, 10, e70092. https://doi.org/10.7554/eLife.70092 Mölle, M., Marshall, L., Gais, S., & Born, J. (2002). Grouping spindle activity slow oscillations human non-rapid eye movement sleep. Journal Neuroscience, 22(24), 10941–10947.

what: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
text: Raw polysomnography signal analysis sleep circadian research.

title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
text: mrpheus deepest layer Circadia Lab R ecosystem — ingests raw multi-channel PSG recordings (EDF/EDF+) produces two outputs feed everything downstream: staged hypnograms (passed hypnor) PSG-derived metrics (passed syncR). name spelled Morpheus carries silent m, pronounced Orpheus — portmanteau myths simultaneously. Morpheus, god dreams, gives package subject matter. Orpheus, descended underworld navigate unconscious, gives spirit. Sleep . “calmed savage beasts song, mrpheus calms raw signals stages.”

title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
text: package ships data object palette_orpheus — 8 colours extracted Roman mosaic Orpheus Charming Animals (3rd century AD, Palermo Archaeological Museum), image inspired package name:
code: scales::show_col(mrpheus::palette_orpheus)

title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
text: 📂 EDF/EDF+ ingestion — read_edf() / prepare_psg(): channel inventory, epoch segmentation, bad-channel flagging 🚫 Artefact detection — detect_artifacts(): amplitude muscle contamination flagging 📊 Spectral analysis — compute_band_power() (δ/θ/α/σ/β/γ per epoch), compute_spectrogram() 🔁 Sleep event detection — compute_spindles(), compute_slow_oscillations() 🛏️ Automatic AASM staging — stage_epochs(): uses pre-trained LightGBM model ported YASA (Vallat & Walker, 2021); see Staging model 🫁 Respiratory metrics — detect_apneas(), compute_ahi(), compute_odi() 💓 Cardiac HRV — compute_hrv_sleep(): HRV stratified sleep stage 🔗 Ecosystem handoffs — export_hypnogram() → hypnor; PSG metrics → syncR

title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
code: mrpheus/ ├── R/ │   ├── mrpheus-package.R       # package doc + palette_orpheus data doc │   ├── read_edf.R              # read_edf(), print.mrpheus_edf │   ├── prepare_psg.R           # prepare_psg(), print.mrpheus_psg │   ├── detect_artifacts.R      # detect_artifacts() │   ├── compute_band_power.R    # compute_band_power() │   ├── compute_spectrogram.R   # compute_spectrogram() │   ├── compute_spindles.R      # compute_spindles() │   ├── compute_slow_oscillations.R │   ├── stage_epochs.R          # stage_epochs(), .extract_staging_features() │   ├── export_hypnogram.R      # export_hypnogram() │   ├── detect_apneas.R         # detect_apneas(), compute_ahi(), compute_odi() │   └── compute_hrv_sleep.R     # compute_hrv_sleep() ├── inst/models/ │   └── yasa_staging.txt        # serialised LightGBM model (see data-raw/) ├── data-raw/ │   ├── fetch_yasa_model.py     # extracts model from YASA Python package │   └── palette_orpheus.R       # generates palette_orpheus data object ├── tests/testthat/ ├── _pkgdown.yml └── DESCRIPTION

title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
code: # R >= 4.1 install.packages(c("edfReader", "gsignal", "pracma", "lightgbm",                    "dplyr", "tibble", "cli", "rlang"))

title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
code: remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/mrpheus")

title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
text: LightGBM staging model bundled repo default (requires Python + YASA extract). Run cloning: model weights cross-language — model serialised Python’s lgb.Booster.save_model() loads identically R’s lightgbm::lgb.load().
code: pip install yasa lightgbm python data-raw/fetch_yasa_model.py # then: usethis::use_data() or copy to inst/models/yasa_staging.txt

title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
code: library(mrpheus)  # 1. Ingest rec <- read_edf("psg_001.edf") psg <- prepare_psg(rec)  # 2. Artefacts art <- detect_artifacts(psg)  # 3. Band power bp  <- compute_band_power(psg, relative = TRUE)  # 4. Events sp  <- compute_spindles(psg) so  <- compute_slow_oscillations(psg)  # 5. Stage staging <- stage_epochs(psg, artefacts = art)  # 6. Export to hypnor hypnogram <- export_hypnogram(staging, start_time = rec$header$startTime)

title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R
text: 🌊 zeitR — wrist actigraphy analysis circadian metrics 😴 hypnor — hypnogram handling, plotting, architecture metrics 🔗 syncR — unified participant-indexed database (actigraphy + sleep diary + PSG) 📋 tallieR — sociodemographics questionnaires 📓 slumbR — sleep diary processing 🎨 circadia — shared visual identity (palettes, themes) 🔬 circadia-bio — Circadia Lab GitHub organisation

title: mrpheus — Polysomnography signal analysis for R

title: Compute Apnea-Hypopnea Index (AHI) — compute_ahi
text: Calculates AHI respiratory event data total sleep time.

title: Compute Apnea-Hypopnea Index (AHI) — compute_ahi
code: compute_ahi(events, tst_hours)

title: Compute Apnea-Hypopnea Index (AHI) — compute_ahi
text: events Output detect_apneas(). tst_hours Numeric. Total sleep time hours.

title: Compute Apnea-Hypopnea Index (AHI) — compute_ahi
text: Numeric scalar. AHI (events per hour).

what: Compute EEG band power per epoch — compute_band_power
title: Compute EEG band power per epoch — compute_band_power
text: Estimates power spectral density (PSD) using Welch's method integrates power within standard EEG frequency bands (delta, theta, alpha, sigma, beta, gamma) epoch specified channel. Mirrors band-power feature extraction used YASA's staging pipeline.

title: Compute EEG band power per epoch — compute_band_power
code: compute_band_power(   psg,   channels = NULL,   bands = list(delta = c(0.5, 4), theta = c(4, 8), alpha = c(8, 13), sigma = c(13, 16),     beta = c(16, 30), gamma = c(30, 40)),   relative = FALSE,   window_s = 4,   overlap = 0.5 )

title: Compute EEG band power per epoch — compute_band_power
text: psg mrpheus_psg object prepare_psg(). channels Character vector. EEG channel labels. NULL (default), non-bad EEG channels used. bands Named list length-2 numeric vectors defining frequency bands. Default:   relative Logical. TRUE, return relative band power (band / total power 0.5–40 Hz). Default FALSE. window_s Numeric. Welch window length seconds. Default 4. overlap Numeric [0, 1). Fractional overlap Welch windows. Default 0.5.
code: list(delta = c(0.5, 4), theta = c(4, 8), alpha = c(8, 13),      sigma = c(13, 16), beta = c(16, 30), gamma = c(30, 40))

title: Compute EEG band power per epoch — compute_band_power
text: tibble columns epoch, channel, one column per band, total_power. Units µV²/Hz (dimensionless relative = TRUE).

title: Compute EEG band power per epoch — compute_band_power
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ bp <- compute_band_power(psg) bp <- compute_band_power(psg, channels = "EEG Fpz-Cz", relative = TRUE) } # }

what: Compute HRV metrics across sleep stages — compute_hrv_sleep
title: Compute HRV metrics across sleep stages — compute_hrv_sleep
text: Extracts R-peak positions ECG channel, computes standard time- frequency-domain heart rate variability (HRV) metrics, stratifies results sleep stage.

title: Compute HRV metrics across sleep stages — compute_hrv_sleep
code: compute_hrv_sleep(   psg,   staging = NULL,   ecg_channel = NULL,   min_rr_ms = 300,   max_rr_ms = 2000 )

title: Compute HRV metrics across sleep stages — compute_hrv_sleep
text: psg mrpheus_psg object prepare_psg(). staging Tibble stage_epochs() NULL. NULL, HRV computed across epochs without stage stratification. ecg_channel Character NULL. ECG channel label. NULL (default), first non-bad ECG channel used. min_rr_ms Numeric. Minimum physiologically plausible RR interval (ms). Default 300 (200 bpm ceiling). max_rr_ms Numeric. Maximum physiologically plausible RR interval (ms). Default 2000 (30 bpm floor).

title: Compute HRV metrics across sleep stages — compute_hrv_sleep
text: tibble one row per sleep stage (one row staging NULL): stage Character. AASM stage "". n_epochs Integer. Number epochs stage. mean_rr_ms Numeric. Mean RR interval (ms). sdnn_ms Numeric. SDNN — SD NN intervals. rmssd_ms Numeric. RMSSD — root mean square successive differences. lf_power Numeric. LF band power (0.04–0.15 Hz). hf_power Numeric. HF band power (0.15–0.4 Hz). lf_hf_ratio Numeric. LF/HF ratio.

title: Compute Oxygen Desaturation Index (ODI) — compute_odi
text: Calculates ODI (number >= 3% SpO2 desaturations per hour sleep) SpO2 channel.

title: Compute Oxygen Desaturation Index (ODI) — compute_odi
code: compute_odi(psg, spo2_channel, tst_hours, threshold = 3)

title: Compute Oxygen Desaturation Index (ODI) — compute_odi
text: psg mrpheus_psg object prepare_psg(). spo2_channel Character. SpO2 channel label. tst_hours Numeric. Total sleep time hours. threshold Numeric. Desaturation threshold (percentage points). Default 3.

title: Compute Oxygen Desaturation Index (ODI) — compute_odi
text: Numeric scalar. ODI (desaturations per hour).

title: Detect slow oscillations — compute_slow_oscillations
text: Detects slow oscillations (SOs) EEG channels using zero-crossing approach delta band (0.5–2 Hz), following algorithm described Mölle et al. (2002) implemented YASA (Vallat & Walker, 2021).

title: Detect slow oscillations — compute_slow_oscillations
code: compute_slow_oscillations(   psg,   channel = NULL,   stages = NULL,   freq_so = c(0.5, 2),   amp_ptp_threshold_uv = c(75, 500),   duration_pos_s = c(0.1, 1),   duration_neg_s = c(0.1, 1.5) )

title: Detect slow oscillations — compute_slow_oscillations
text: psg mrpheus_psg object prepare_psg(). channel Character. EEG channel label. NULL (default), first non-bad EEG channel used. stages Integer vector NULL. Epoch indices restricted N2/N3. NULL, detection runs across epochs. freq_so Numeric vector length 2. frequency band (Hz). Default c(0.5, 2). amp_ptp_threshold_uv Numeric vector length 2. Min max acceptable peak--peak amplitude (µV). Default c(75, 500). duration_pos_s Numeric vector length 2. Min max positive half- wave duration (s). Default c(0.1, 1.0). duration_neg_s Numeric vector length 2. Min max negative half- wave duration (s). Default c(0.1, 1.5).

title: Detect slow oscillations — compute_slow_oscillations
text: tibble one row per detected slow oscillation: epoch Integer. start_s Numeric. Onset (s) relative epoch start. end_s Numeric. Offset (s) relative epoch start. duration_s Numeric. neg_peak_uv Numeric. Negative peak amplitude (µV). pos_peak_uv Numeric. Positive peak amplitude (µV). ptp_uv Numeric. Peak--peak amplitude (µV). channel Character.

title: Detect slow oscillations — compute_slow_oscillations
text: Mölle, M., Marshall, L., Gais, S., & Born, J. (2002). Grouping spindle activity slow oscillations human non-rapid eye movement sleep. Journal Neuroscience, 22(24), 10941–10947. Vallat, R., & Walker, M. P. (2021). open-source, high-performance tool automated sleep staging. eLife, 10, e70092. doi:10.7554/eLife.70092

title: Compute a time-frequency spectrogram — compute_spectrogram
text: Generates short-time Fourier transform (STFT) based spectrogram single PSG channel across epochs. Returns power (µV²/Hz) matrix time (epochs) rows frequency columns.

title: Compute a time-frequency spectrogram — compute_spectrogram
code: compute_spectrogram(   psg,   channel = NULL,   freq_range = c(0, 40),   window_s = 2,   overlap = 0.5,   db = TRUE )

title: Compute a time-frequency spectrogram — compute_spectrogram
text: psg mrpheus_psg object prepare_psg(). channel Character. single channel label. freq_range Numeric vector length 2. Frequency range return (Hz). Default c(0, 40). window_s Numeric. STFT window length seconds. Default 2. overlap Numeric [0, 1). Fractional window overlap. Default 0.5. db Logical. Return power decibels (10 * log10(power))? Default TRUE.

title: Compute a time-frequency spectrogram — compute_spectrogram
text: list class mrpheus_spectrogram : power Numeric matrix. Rows = epochs, columns = frequency bins. freqs Numeric vector. Frequency bin centres (Hz). epochs Integer vector. Epoch indices. channel Character. channel label. db Logical. Whether power dB.

title: Detect sleep spindles — compute_spindles
text: Detects sleep spindles EEG channels using band-pass / RMS envelope approach, closely following algorithm Lacourse et al. (2019) implemented YASA (Vallat & Walker, 2021). Spindles identified transient bursts 11–16 Hz activity NREM sleep.

title: Detect sleep spindles — compute_spindles
code: compute_spindles(   psg,   channel = NULL,   stages = NULL,   freq_spindle = c(11, 16),   rms_window_s = 0.3,   min_duration_s = 0.5,   max_duration_s = 3,   threshold_sd = 1.5 )

title: Detect sleep spindles — compute_spindles
text: psg mrpheus_psg object prepare_psg(). channel Character. EEG channel label. NULL (default), first non-bad EEG channel used. stages Integer vector NULL. Epoch indices (1-based) restricted NREM sleep. NULL, spindles detected across epochs. freq_spindle Numeric vector length 2. Spindle frequency band (Hz). Default c(11, 16). rms_window_s Numeric. Moving RMS window length seconds. Default 0.3. min_duration_s Numeric. Minimum spindle duration seconds. Default 0.5. max_duration_s Numeric. Maximum spindle duration seconds. Default 3.0. threshold_sd Numeric. RMS threshold multiple channel SD (within sigma band). Default 1.5.

title: Detect sleep spindles — compute_spindles
text: tibble one row per detected spindle: epoch Integer. Epoch spindle detected. start_s Numeric. Spindle onset relative epoch start (seconds). end_s Numeric. Spindle offset relative epoch start (seconds). duration_s Numeric. Spindle duration seconds. peak_freq_hz Numeric. Peak frequency within spindle. rms_uv Numeric. Mean RMS amplitude within spindle (µV). channel Character. Channel label.

title: Detect sleep spindles — compute_spindles
text: Lacourse, K., Yetton, B., Mednick, S., & Bhatt, D. L. (2019). Massive online sleep staging: polysomnography data repository. Journal Sleep Research. Vallat, R., & Walker, M. P. (2021). open-source, high-performance tool automated sleep staging. eLife, 10, e70092. doi:10.7554/eLife.70092

what: Detect respiratory events (apneas and hypopneas) — detect_apneas
title: Detect respiratory events (apneas and hypopneas) — detect_apneas
text: Detects apneas hypopneas respiratory airflow effort signals, returning event-level metadata summary indices. Detection follows AASM 2012/2017 criteria: >= 90% signal reduction >= 10 s (apnea), >= 30% reduction >= 3% SpO2 desaturation arousal >= 10 s (hypopnea).

title: Detect respiratory events (apneas and hypopneas) — detect_apneas
code: detect_apneas(   psg,   airflow_channel = NULL,   spo2_channel = NULL,   min_duration_s = 10,   apnea_threshold = 0.9,   hypopnea_threshold = 0.3,   desaturation_threshold = 3 )

title: Detect respiratory events (apneas and hypopneas) — detect_apneas
text: psg mrpheus_psg object prepare_psg(). airflow_channel Character. Airflow channel label (e.g. nasal pressure thermistor). NULL (default), first RESP channel used. spo2_channel Character NULL. SpO2 channel hypopnea scoring. min_duration_s Numeric. Minimum event duration seconds. Default 10. apnea_threshold Numeric. Fractional reduction required apnea classification. Default 0.90. hypopnea_threshold Numeric. Fractional reduction required hypopnea classification. Default 0.30. desaturation_threshold Numeric. SpO2 drop (percentage points) required confirm hypopnea. Default 3.

title: Detect respiratory events (apneas and hypopneas) — detect_apneas
text: list : events Tibble. One row per event: epoch, start_s, end_s, duration_s, type ("apnea" / "hypopnea"), desaturation. summary Tibble. n_apneas, n_hypopneas, n_events.

what: Detect artefact epochs in a PSG recording — detect_artifacts
title: Detect artefact epochs in a PSG recording — detect_artifacts
text: Flags epochs containing likely artefacts based amplitude thresholds, excessive high-frequency (muscle) power, movement contamination. Operates EEG channels default. Returns logical vector (one value per epoch) optionally attaches per-epoch artefact metrics inspection.

title: Detect artefact epochs in a PSG recording — detect_artifacts
code: detect_artifacts(   psg,   channels = NULL,   amp_threshold_uv = 500,   hf_band = c(40, 100),   hf_percentile = 0.99,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Detect artefact epochs in a PSG recording — detect_artifacts
text: psg mrpheus_psg object prepare_psg(). channels Character vector. Channel labels evaluate. NULL (default), non-bad EEG channels used. amp_threshold_uv Numeric. Peak--peak amplitude threshold µV. Epochs exceeding selected channel flagged. Default 500. hf_band Numeric vector length 2. Frequency band (Hz) considered high-frequency / muscle contamination. Default c(40, 100). hf_percentile Numeric. Epochs whose HF power exceeds percentile (across epochs) flagged. Default 0.99. verbose Logical. Print summary. Default TRUE.

title: Detect artefact epochs in a PSG recording — detect_artifacts
text: tibble one row per epoch columns: epoch Integer. Epoch index (1-based). artefact Logical. TRUE epoch flagged artefact. reason Character. Comma-separated reasons flagging, NA. peak_to_peak_uv Numeric. Maximum peak--peak amplitude across selected channels. hf_power_db Numeric. Mean HF band power (dB) across selected channels.

what: Export a staged hypnogram to hypnor — export_hypnogram
title: Export a staged hypnogram to hypnor — export_hypnogram
text: Converts staging tibble produced stage_epochs() hypnor_hypnogram object ready architecture metric computation visualisation hypnor package. primary handoff mrpheus rest Circadia Lab ecosystem.

title: Export a staged hypnogram to hypnor — export_hypnogram
code: export_hypnogram(   staging,   epoch_s = 30,   start_time = NULL,   participant_id = NULL )

title: Export a staged hypnogram to hypnor — export_hypnogram
text: staging tibble stage_epochs() columns epoch, stage, optional probability columns. epoch_s Numeric. Epoch duration seconds. Must match epoch_s used prepare_psg(). Default 30. start_time POSIXct NULL. Recording start time. Used compute clock-time axes hypnor plots. NULL, epochs indexed 0. participant_id Character NULL. Optional identifier passed hypnor syncR.

title: Export a staged hypnogram to hypnor — export_hypnogram
text: object class hypnor_hypnogram (defined hypnor). hypnor installed, returns staging tibble invisibly message.

what: mrpheus: Polysomnography Signal Analysis for Sleep Research — mrpheus-package
title: mrpheus: Polysomnography Signal Analysis for Sleep Research — mrpheus-package
text: Raw polysomnography (PSG) signal analysis sleep circadian research. Provides EDF/EDF+ ingestion, artefact detection, spectral analysis, sleep event detection (spindles, slow oscillations), automatic AASM sleep staging via pre-trained LightGBM model (ported YASA; Vallat & Walker, 2021), respiratory cardiac metrics. Exports staged hypnograms directly hypnor PSG-derived metrics syncR. Part Circadia Lab R ecosystem.

title: mrpheus: Polysomnography Signal Analysis for Sleep Research — mrpheus-package
text: Maintainer: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk Authors: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk Mario Leocadio-Miguel (ORCID)

title: Orpheus mosaic palette — palette_orpheus
text: 8-colour palette extracted Roman mosaic Orpheus Charming Animals (3rd century AD, Palermo Archaeological Museum). mosaic depicts Orpheus — mythological figure whose name story package honours — surrounded animals, rendered warm Mediterranean tesserae.

title: Orpheus mosaic palette — palette_orpheus
code: palette_orpheus

title: Orpheus mosaic palette — palette_orpheus

title: Orpheus mosaic palette — palette_orpheus
text: Mosaic: Orpheus Charming Animals, Roman, 3rd century AD. Palermo Archaeological Museum. Image via Wikimedia Commons, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orpheus.

title: Orpheus mosaic palette — palette_orpheus

title: Orpheus mosaic palette — palette_orpheus

what: Prepare a PSG recording for analysis — prepare_psg
title: Prepare a PSG recording for analysis — prepare_psg
text: Takes mrpheus_edf object segments standard epochs, performs channel inventory (classifying signals type), flags channels appear flat likely bad. standard entry point downstream analyses (spectral, event detection, staging).

title: Prepare a PSG recording for analysis — prepare_psg
code: prepare_psg(   edf,   epoch_s = 30,   eeg_pattern = "EEG|C3|C4|F3|F4|O1|O2|Fpz|Pz",   eog_pattern = "EOG|ROC|LOC",   emg_pattern = "EMG|chin|Chin",   ecg_pattern = "ECG|EKG",   resp_pattern = "Thor|Abdo|Flow|SpO2|airflow",   flat_threshold = 1e-06 )

title: Prepare a PSG recording for analysis — prepare_psg
text: edf mrpheus_edf object read_edf(). epoch_s Numeric. Epoch length seconds. Default 30 (standard AASM epoch). eeg_pattern Character. Regex pattern identify EEG channels. Default "EEG|C3|C4|F3|F4|O1|O2|Fpz|Pz". eog_pattern Character. Regex pattern identify EOG channels. Default "EOG|ROC|LOC". emg_pattern Character. Regex pattern identify EMG channels. Default "EMG|chin|Chin". ecg_pattern Character. Regex pattern identify ECG/EKG channels. Default "ECG|EKG". resp_pattern Character. Regex pattern identify respiratory channels. Default "Thor|Abdo|Flow|SpO2|airflow". flat_threshold Numeric. Variance value flags channel flat/bad. Default 1e-6.

title: Prepare a PSG recording for analysis — prepare_psg
text: list class mrpheus_psg components: edf original mrpheus_edf object. epochs List. element one epoch (30 s default), named list channel vectors. n_epochs Integer. Total number complete epochs. epoch_s Numeric. Epoch duration seconds. channel_map Data frame. Channel label, detected type (EEG/EOG/EMG/ECG/RESP/), sample rate, bad flag.

title: Prepare a PSG recording for analysis — prepare_psg
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec  <- read_edf("data/psg_001.edf") psg  <- prepare_psg(rec) psg$channel_map } # }

what: Read an EDF or EDF+ recording — read_edf
title: Read an EDF or EDF+ recording — read_edf
text: Reads European Data Format (EDF EDF+) file returns structured list containing signal data, channel metadata, recording header. Wraps edfReader::readEdfHeader() edfReader::readEdfSignals() consistent output formatting mrpheus pipeline.

title: Read an EDF or EDF+ recording — read_edf
code: read_edf(path, channels = NULL, only_header = FALSE)

title: Read an EDF or EDF+ recording — read_edf
text: path Character. Path .edf .edf+ file. channels Character vector NULL. Channel labels import. NULL (default), channels imported. only_header Logical. TRUE, return header without reading signal data. Useful quick channel inspection. Default FALSE.

title: Read an EDF or EDF+ recording — read_edf
text: list class mrpheus_edf components: header Data frame. Recording metadata (patient info, start time, number signals, etc.). signals Named list numeric vectors, one per channel. channels Data frame. Channel-level metadata: label, sample rate, physical min/max, digital min/max, transducer type, prefiltering. duration_s Numeric. Total recording duration seconds. path Character. Resolved path source file.

title: Read an EDF or EDF+ recording — read_edf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rec <- read_edf("data/psg_001.edf") rec <- read_edf("data/psg_001.edf", channels = c("EEG Fpz-Cz", "EOG horizontal")) rec$channels } # }

title: Automatic AASM sleep staging — stage_epochs
text: Stages 30-second epoch using pre-trained LightGBM model originally developed YASA (Vallat & Walker, 2021) shipped cross-language serialised model inst/models/yasa_staging.txt. Features computed R match Python feature extraction pipeline exactly; bit-exact parity validated package test suite.

title: Automatic AASM sleep staging — stage_epochs
code: stage_epochs(   psg,   eeg_channel = NULL,   eog_channel = NULL,   emg_channel = NULL,   artefacts = NULL,   model_path = system.file("models", "yasa_staging.txt", package = "mrpheus") )

title: Automatic AASM sleep staging — stage_epochs
text: psg mrpheus_psg object prepare_psg(). eeg_channel Character. Central EEG channel (e.g. "EEG Fpz-Cz"). NULL (default), first non-bad EEG channel used. eog_channel Character NULL. EOG channel. NULL (default), first non-bad EOG channel used (EOG features omitted none found). emg_channel Character NULL. Chin EMG channel. NULL (default), first non-bad EMG channel used (EMG features omitted none found). artefacts Tibble NULL. Output detect_artifacts(). Artefact epochs assigned NA stage excluded model. NULL, epochs staged. model_path Character. Path serialised LightGBM model. Defaults bundled model system.file("models/yasa_staging.txt", package = "mrpheus").

title: Automatic AASM sleep staging — stage_epochs
text: tibble one row per epoch: epoch Integer. stage Character. AASM stage: W, N1, N2, N3, REM, NA (artefact). prob_W, prob_N1, prob_N2, prob_N3, prob_REM Numeric. Posterior class probabilities LightGBM model.

title: Automatic AASM sleep staging — stage_epochs
text: Stages returned follow standard AASM nomenclature: W (wake), N1, N2, N3, REM.

title: Automatic AASM sleep staging — stage_epochs
text: Vallat, R., & Walker, M. P. (2021). open-source, high-performance tool automated sleep staging. eLife, 10, e70092. doi:10.7554/eLife.70092

title: mrpheus (development version)
text: Package scaffolded full PSG analysis pipeline. read_edf() / prepare_psg() — EDF/EDF+ ingestion, channel inventory, epoch segmentation, bad-channel flagging. detect_artifacts() — amplitude high-frequency artefact detection. compute_band_power() — Welch PSD δ/θ/α/σ/β/γ bands per epoch. compute_spectrogram() — STFT-based time-frequency spectrogram. compute_spindles() — RMS envelope spindle detection (Lacourse et al., 2019). compute_slow_oscillations() — zero-crossing detection (Mölle et al., 2002). stage_epochs() — automatic AASM staging via pre-trained LightGBM model (ported YASA; Vallat & Walker, 2021). Feature extraction parity Python pipeline progress. export_hypnogram() — handoff hypnor. detect_apneas(), compute_ahi(), compute_odi() — respiratory stubs. compute_hrv_sleep() — HRV stub. palette_orpheus — 8-colour palette extracted Roman mosaic Orpheus Charming Animals (3rd century AD, Palermo Archaeological Museum). Hex sticker: olive background, ivory lyre, ochre motion lines, vermillion border. pkgdown site deploying via Netlify mrpheus.circadia-lab.uk.
